test_cases.title: Inviting a student sends an email with a unique invite link
test_cases.test_scenario: Verify an invite email with a unique link is sent when an instructor invites a student
test_cases.preconditions: Instructor is logged in and owns a course
steps: Log in as Instructor
Open a course and invite student email 'student@example.com'
Check the email inbox for student@example.com
test_cases.expected_result: An email is delivered containing a unique, single-use invite link for the course

test_cases.title: Unauthenticated student opening invite link sees login screen with course context
test_cases.test_scenario: Verify no-session student sees login/register with a course context banner
test_cases.preconditions: No active session in browser; invite link is valid
steps: Open a valid invite link with no active session
Observe the screen shown
test_cases.expected_result: Login/register screen shown with a banner naming the course and instructor above the form

test_cases.title: Successful login returns student to the Course Invite screen
test_cases.test_scenario: Verify join intent is preserved — post-login the student lands on the invite screen, not the dashboard
test_cases.preconditions: Student completed OTP verification from within the invite flow
steps: Open invite link without a session
Complete login via OTP
Observe the post-login destination
test_cases.expected_result: Student is returned to the Course Invite screen for the original invite, not the generic dashboard

test_cases.title: Valid invite shows course name, instructor, start date, and Enroll CTA
test_cases.test_scenario: Verify the Course Invite screen displays all required course details
test_cases.preconditions: Student is logged in; invite link is valid and unclaimed
steps: Open a valid invite link while logged in
Observe the Course Invite screen
test_cases.test_data: course=Intro to Statistics; instructor=Dr. Rao; start_date=2026-08-01
test_cases.expected_result: Screen shows course name, instructor name, start date, and an Enroll CTA

test_cases.title: Invalid invite token shows invalid error state
test_cases.test_scenario: Verify a malformed or non-existent invite token shows the invalid state
test_cases.preconditions: Student opens an invite URL with a non-existent token
steps: Open invite URL with token=INVALID123
Observe the screen
test_cases.expected_result: 'This invite link is invalid.' message shown with a Go Home CTA; no course details exposed

test_cases.title: Invite expires at exactly the 14-day boundary
test_cases.test_scenario: Verify an invite created exactly 14 days ago is treated as expired
steps: Open invite link with created_at exactly 14 days ago
Observe the resulting screen
test_cases.expected_result: Expired state shown; invite is rejected at the 14-day boundary

test_cases.title: Invite valid at 13 days old — one day before expiry
test_cases.test_scenario: Verify an invite at 13 days old is still valid (boundary: one day before 14-day expiry)
steps: Open invite link with created_at exactly 13 days ago
Observe the resulting screen
test_cases.expected_result: Course Invite screen shown; invite is treated as valid; no expired state

test_cases.title: Already-claimed invite shows already-used state
test_cases.test_scenario: Verify an invite already used to enroll shows the already-claimed state
test_cases.preconditions: Invite token has already been claimed by a previous successful enrollment
steps: Open an invite link that was previously used to enroll
test_cases.expected_result: 'This invite link has already been used.' message shown with a Go Home CTA; no Enroll CTA visible

test_cases.title: Already-enrolled student opening invite sees already-enrolled state
test_cases.test_scenario: Verify a student already enrolled in the course sees the already-enrolled state, not the Enroll CTA
test_cases.preconditions: Student is already enrolled in the course; student is logged in
steps: Open a valid invite link for a course the student is already enrolled in
test_cases.expected_result: 'You're already enrolled in <Course Name>.' state shown; no Enroll CTA present

test_cases.title: Tapping Enroll creates enrollment and marks invite as claimed
test_cases.test_scenario: Verify the happy-path enroll: enrollment created, invite claimed, success state shown
test_cases.preconditions: Student logged in; invite valid; not already enrolled
steps: Open a valid invite
Tap the Enroll CTA
test_cases.expected_result: Enrollment record created; invite marked as claimed; success state shown with course name and a 'Start Learning' CTA

test_cases.title: Enroll API failure shows retryable error, invite not claimed
test_cases.test_scenario: Verify enroll API failure shows an error and keeps the invite token unclaimed
steps: Tap the Enroll CTA
API returns 500
test_cases.expected_result: 'Unable to enroll. Please try again.' shown; Retry CTA available; invite token NOT marked claimed

test_cases.title: Enroll CTA disabled during in-flight request to prevent duplicate submissions
test_cases.test_scenario: Verify double-tapping Enroll does not send duplicate enrollment requests
test_cases.preconditions: Student taps Enroll; request is in-flight (slow network simulated)
steps: Tap the Enroll CTA
Immediately tap it again before the response arrives
test_cases.expected_result: Only one POST /enrollments request is sent; CTA shows a loading state and is non-interactive; no duplicate enrollment created

test_cases.title: Backend rejects duplicate enrollment even if UI is bypassed
test_cases.test_scenario: Verify the enroll API returns an error when the student is already enrolled
test_cases.preconditions: Student is already enrolled; enroll API called directly
steps: Student already enrolled in the course
Call POST /enrollments directly for the same course
Observe API response
test_cases.expected_result: API returns 409 or equivalent error; no duplicate enrollment created; enrolled-student count unchanged

test_cases.title: Enroll CTA has an accessible label and is keyboard operable
test_cases.test_scenario: Verify the Enroll CTA can be reached and activated via keyboard and announces a descriptive label
test_cases.preconditions: Screen reader active; Course Invite screen displayed
steps: Tab to the Enroll CTA
Confirm the announced accessible label
Activate it with Enter/Space
test_cases.expected_result: Enroll CTA is reachable via keyboard, announces a course-specific accessible label, and activates enrollment on Enter/Space

test_cases.title: Error states are announced assertively to screen readers
test_cases.test_scenario: Verify invalid/expired/claimed/error states are announced via an assertive aria-live region
test_cases.preconditions: Screen reader active; each invite error state triggered in turn
steps: Trigger the invalid state and listen to the announcement
Trigger the expired state and listen
Trigger the enroll-failure state and listen
test_cases.expected_result: Each error state is announced immediately and assertively; focus moves to the error heading in each case

test_cases.title: Course Invite screen and Enroll API meet the P95 latency SLA
test_cases.test_scenario: Verify both the invite screen load and the enroll API response meet the 2000ms P95 SLA
test_cases.preconditions: Performance testing environment configured; load testing tool ready
steps: Load the Course Invite screen over a simulated 4G connection 50 times
Measure P95 screen load time
Fire 50 concurrent Enroll requests and measure P95 API response time
test_cases.expected_result: P95 screen load time <= 2000ms; P95 enroll API response time <= 2000ms

test_cases.title: E2E happy path: invite email to enrolled success state
test_cases.test_scenario: E2E: complete flow from receiving the invite email through to a successful enrollment
test_cases.preconditions: Instructor has invited student@example.com to a course; student has no existing session
steps: Instructor invites student@example.com to the course
Open the invite link from the received email with no active session
Log in via OTP
Verify the Course Invite screen shows correct course details
Tap the Enroll CTA
Verify enrollment success state and that the invite is marked claimed
test_cases.expected_result: All steps succeed; enrollment exists; invite is claimed; success state shown with a Start Learning CTA

test_cases.title: Invite endpoint rejects requests without a valid auth token
test_cases.test_scenario: Security: verify the enroll endpoint requires authentication
steps: Send POST /enrollments without a valid auth token
Observe API response
test_cases.expected_result: API returns 401 Unauthorized; no enrollment is created

gaps.requirement_text: Instructor invites a student to a course by email; system sends an email containing a unique invite link.
gaps.issue: No behavior is defined for inviting an email address that is already registered as a student vs. one that has never signed up. It's unclear whether the email content or login step differs between these two cases.
gaps.suggested_fix: Specify whether the invite email/flow differs for existing accounts (direct login prompt) vs. new signups (registration prompt), and what happens if the email is already registered under a different role.

gaps.requirement_text: Invite link states: invalid, expired (>14 days), already claimed.
gaps.issue: Behavior is undefined when the course itself is deleted or archived after the invite was sent but before it's opened.
gaps.suggested_fix: Define a distinct 'course unavailable' state (separate from invalid/expired/claimed) for invites pointing to a deleted or archived course.

gaps.requirement_text: Tapping Enroll creates the enrollment and marks the invite as claimed.
gaps.issue: Order of operations between marking the invite claimed and creating the enrollment record is not specified, so it's unclear which write happens first and what the recovery behavior is if the second write fails after the first succeeds.
gaps.suggested_fix: Specify the required order (e.g., enrollment created first, then invite marked claimed only on success) and the retry behavior for a partial failure between the two writes.

gaps.requirement_text: System sends an email containing a unique invite link.
gaps.issue: No rate limit or cooldown is specified for an instructor re-sending an invite to the same email/course repeatedly.
gaps.suggested_fix: Define a resend cooldown (e.g., 60 seconds) and a cap on invites per email/course per day to prevent spam.

security_checks: Verify duplicate enrollment is rejected server-side even if the UI is bypassed (TC-013)
Verify the enroll endpoint requires a valid auth token (TC-018)
accessibility_checks: Enroll CTA has an accessible label and is keyboard operable (TC-014)
Error states are announced assertively to screen readers (TC-015)
performance_checks: Course Invite screen and Enroll API meet the 2000ms P95 SLA (TC-016)
exploratory_ideas: Open the same invite link in two browser tabs and enroll from both simultaneously
Invite the same email to the same course twice before the first invite is opened
Test session expiry between opening the invite and tapping Enroll
Archive the course after sending the invite but before the student opens it
